patch.value: preflight: data-sanity gate (hard-fail on dependency cycles, warn on soft issues)

Register a preflight data-sanity validation gate that runs BEFORE the gantt/schedule render so bad data surfaces early instead of producing a silently-confusing chart. Hard-fails (CommandError, non-zero exit) only on truly render-breaking conditions (dependency cycles); warns to stderr (non-blocking, chart still renders) on soft issues (deadline-before-start, implausibly large estimate). Normal valid data produces no warnings and no block.

value.author: codex
value.text: FAIL-vs-WARN policy: HARD-FAIL only on a dependency CYCLE — a cycle has no valid topological order, so forward scheduling is impossible and the existing render-path cycle guards would silently break the cycle and emit a plausible-but-wrong chart (the confusing-data case this gate stops). WARN (non-blocking, stderr) on soft issues that still yield a useful chart: deadline-before-its-own-start, and an implausibly-large estimate (>2000h, likely minutes-vs-hours error). Deliberately did NOT hard-fail or duplicate the infeasible/unreachable-deadline check — round-3 already FLAGS that in the render via infeasibleWarnings; chart still useful so it stays a warning. Gate kept judicious so normal charts never block.

value.author: codex
value.text: SDK swallow-throw finding (verified functionally): pm runtime wraps registerPreflight overrides in try/catch and downgrades a thrown error to a non-fatal warning, so a throw inside registerPreflight does NOT abort the command. Therefore the real enforcement lives in the command/exporter HANDLER via runDataSanityGate() which throws the package CommandError(EXIT_CODE.USAGE) to truly fail fast. A scoped pass-through registerPreflight is still registered (returns empty delta, no-op for non-gantt commands) so the extension truthfully advertises the 'preflight' capability. Also kept 'schema' in the manifest because registerCommand declares a flags:[] array (SDK requires 'schema' for flag declarations).

value.author: codex
value.text: FUNCTIONAL EVIDENCE (real pm project /tmp/scratch-gantt, 4 Tasks w/ estimates+deadlines+blocked_by chain, extension installed --project; capabilities activated: commands,importers,preflight,schema). (1) CYCLE pm-ajjf→pm-5jnq→pm-jksp→pm-lz2x→pm-ajjf: 'pm gantt --schedule' HARD-FAILED exit=2, 0 bytes stdout (no chart), stderr named the full cycle path; also fails without --schedule (exit 2). (2) Cycle removed + deadline 2026-06-01 set before created_at 2026-06-04 on pm-ajjf: 'pm gantt --schedule' exit=0, stderr 'NOTE: 1 data-sanity warning(s) (chart still rendered): pm-ajjf Design API: deadline 2026-06-01 is before its start 2026-06-04', 41 lines of chart on stdout. (3) Fully-sane data: 'pm gantt --schedule' exit=0, no stderr, clean chart. Unit tests added (test/preflight.test.ts): 9 cases for detectCycles + dataSanityReport. Full suite 31/31 pass; build + tsc --noEmit clean.

patch.value: Added detectCycles() (3-color DFS, reports each distinct cycle path with id+title), dataSanityReport() ({fatal,warnings}), and runDataSanityGate() invoked at the top of the gantt command handler AND the gantt exporter (over all status-filtered items) BEFORE buildRows. Fatal (cycle) -> throw CommandError(EXIT_CODE.USAGE) to truly fail fast (pm runtime swallows throws from registerPreflight). Soft issues (deadline<start, estimate>2000h) -> stderr NOTE, suppressed under --json. Registered a scoped pass-through registerPreflight for the capability surface. Added 'preflight' to manifest capabilities; kept 'schema' (commands declare flags[]). Did not duplicate/hard-fail the existing render-side infeasible-deadline flagging.

Dependency cycle -> clear fail-fast error naming the cycle + non-zero exit, no chart rendered. Soft data issue -> stderr warning but chart still renders with exit 0. Fully-sane data -> clean render, no warnings, no block.

Cycle: exit 2, 0-byte stdout, stderr named full cycle path (also fails without --schedule). Deadline-before-start: exit 0, stderr NOTE line, 41-line chart on stdout. Sane data: exit 0, no stderr, clean chart. All three confirmed; 31/31 tests pass; tsc clean.
Implemented preflight data-sanity gate: hard-fail on dependency cycles, warn on soft issues; built, typechecked, unit-tested (31/31), and functionally verified end-to-end in a real pm project.
